feed.title: The Right to Read
feed.subtitle: Working papers, briefs, and new additions to the campaign library.

entry.title: Whom Does the City Allow to Read?: Caste, Democracy, and the Public Library in the Indian City (v4.0.0)
entry.summary: Seven Indian cities read along the country's two founding public-library lineages: the Madras cess (Chennai) and the Bombay grant (Mumbai). Between them, the reading commons is kept on paper while it is withdrawn as a public, first from the wards where the most caste-marked readers live.

entry.title: In the library: The Arunachal Pradesh Public Libraries Act, 2009

entry.title: A Consolidated Real Per-Capita Series for Public Library Expenditure in India: A Measurement Note in the Public-Finance Tradition, 2014–15 to 2024–25 (v0.1.1)
entry.summary: What India spends, per person, per year, in real terms, on its public libraries: a consolidated state-plus-Centre series, 2014–15 to 2024–25.

entry.title: The Convergence Blueprint: A Tax-Funded National Public Library Network by 2035 (v0.1.0)
entry.summary: A state-government-led roadmap to a tax-funded national public library network by 2035 — about 0.20% of state revenue, to the Tamil Nadu standard.

entry.title: The Grammar of Refusal: Four Mechanisms of Institutional Contraction in India's Library Sector (v0.1.1)
entry.summary: How a Centrally Sponsored Scheme was unmade by procedure: four mechanisms of institutional contraction, read from the parliamentary record, 2015–2026.

entry.title: From Reader to User: Eighty Years of the Indian Library Association and the Changing Public of the Library, 1942–2019 (v0.1.3)
entry.summary: Three primary archives — eighty years of Indian Library Association proceedings, the central government's library-committee reports, and the state Public Libraries Acts (1948–2009) — read as one drift: how the library's reader became a user, and how that drift kept caste unsayable.

entry.title: The ₹10,000 Library: Gujarat's Public Libraries
entry.summary: Gujarat — where India's modern public-library movement began, in Baroda a century ago — today runs roughly one public library for every 20,000 people, funds nine in ten of them as revocable grants to private societies, and caps the village library at ₹10,000 a year. There is no library cess.

entry.title: In the library: What is the per capita expenditure on public libraries in India? An empirical analysis
entry.summary: Kulkarni et al., What is the per capita expenditure on public libraries in India? An empirical analysis (2025) — added to the campaign reading room, shelf: Scholarship.

entry.title: In the library: Toward reforms: Revisiting public library legislation in Indian states
entry.summary: Kulkarni et al., Toward reforms: Revisiting public library legislation in Indian states (2025) — added to the campaign reading room, shelf: Scholarship.

entry.title: In the library: The Returns to Public Library Investment
entry.summary: Gilpin et al., The Returns to Public Library Investment (2024) — added to the campaign reading room, shelf: Scholarship.
